page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr8i.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6462C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, maintaining a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core density of 8.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation combined with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides predictable compute performance and dense memory resources without the overhead of hyperthreading.

The ecs.hfr8i.large is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require dedicated processing power but do not demand high vCPU counts. Suitable use cases include small-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications with moderate compute requirements, and lightweight data processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr8i.large is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on the KVM hypervisor. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6462C processor clocked at 3.3 GHz, it provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 1.0 thread per core, ensuring predictable performance. The instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-sensitive applications that do not require massive parallel processing. It is well-suited for workloads such as small databases, in-memory caches, and lightweight enterprise applications.
